commit.message:
Feature/deck resize 50 (#44)

* feat: resize all decks to 50 D+H

Expand every player deck, enemy sub-deck, and encounter selection pool
to exactly 50 cards (deck + hand combined), preserving all unique card
entries. No Rust source changes - JSON configs only.

Player decks:
- Combat Attack/Defence/Resource: each independently expanded/shrunk to 50
- Mining/Herbalism/Woodcutting/Fishing/Crafting: scaled proportionally to 50
- Research/Rest: expanded to 50 (all cards in deck)

Enemy/encounter sub-decks:
- Combat enemy attack/defence/resource: hand 10→50
- Mining ore_deck: total hand 20→50
- Herbalism plant_hand: total hand 5→50
- Fishing fish_deck: total hand 18→50
- Crafting enemy_crafting_deck: total deck 20→50
- Research interference_deck: total hand 5→50
- Milestone sub-decks: scaled to 50

Encounter selection pools:
- All 8 discipline pools: hand 2-4→50 each
- Milestone encounter pool: hand 1 each→10 each (50 total)

Test fixes:
- Refactor 10 research tests to use create_test_client_from_json with
  RESEARCH_WIN/RESEARCH_LOSS configs - avoids the encounter-hand
  depletion loop that broke when the pool grew to 350+ cards
- Add tokens_with_combat_insight.json test fixture (CombatInsight=100)
- Update cost assertions to match test config base_insight_cost=1
- Add setup_research_client_with_interference_project helper
- Relax woodcutting durability=0 assertion to allow deck-exhaustion loss

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* some suggestions

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Clean up glob path expansion (#168)

The old `expand_path_pattern` used a fragile sequential prefix-swap list
to reverse-transform glob results back to user-facing paths, and had no
environment variable expansion support.

### Changes

- **Split at last `/`** — separates the pattern into `raw_prefix`
(preserved verbatim for output) and `rhs_pattern` (the glob portion)
- **`expanded_prefix` pipeline** — builds the absolute path used for
globbing by delegating to bash's own filename expansion logic
- **`bash_funcs::expand_filename`** — new function that calls
`bash_expand_filename` from `bashline.c` directly via a symbol declared
in `bash_symbols.rs`; returns the original filename unchanged if
expansion fails
- **New `bash_symbols` declaration** — `bash_expand_filename`
- **Result mapping** — strips `expanded_prefix` from each glob result to
get the relative portion, then prepends `raw_prefix` to preserve the
user's original spelling in completions
- **`PathPatternExpansion` struct** — replaces the `(String,
Vec<(String, String)>)` return type with named fields

### Example

```
pattern: "$HOME/projects/fly*"
  raw_prefix:      "$HOME/projects"
  expanded_prefix: "/home/user/projects"   (after bash filename expansion)
  rhs_pattern:     "fly*"
  glob runs:       "/home/user/projects/fly*"
  result path:     "/home/user/projects/flyline"
  suggestion:      "$HOME/projects/flyline"   ← raw_prefix preserved
```

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>clean up glob path expansion</issue_title>
> <issue_description>Currently, the path is expanded for tilde and if it
is a relative path and we keep track of each expansion.
> 
> This is how it should work:
> - split the pattern at the last / 
> - call the lhs the raw_prefix.
> - create expanded_prefix by doing tilde expansion, then absolute path
expansion, then environment variable expansion.
> - return the raw_prefix, expanded_prefix, and the rhs of the pattern.
> - when doing the glob expansion, it should use expanded_prefix + / +
rhs_pattern. But it should then compute the relative path of the result
compared to expanded_prefix and return raw_prefix + / +
result_relative_path
> </issue_description>
> 
> ## Comments on the Issue (you are @copilot in this section)
> 
> <comments>
> </comments>
> 


</details>

docs: rewrite Balancing track B2–B8 with worktree-isolated iterative approach (#43)

Replace the old B2–B6 (monolithic runner/analysis pipeline) with a new
B2–B8 structure centered on per-discipline worktree isolation:

- B2: General config bypass — manual quick-win optimizations
- B3: Per-discipline runners in isolated worktrees/branches
- B4: Per-discipline analysis with mutation proposals
- B5: Mutation validation runners in separate worktrees
- B6: Manual iteration loop (alternate B4↔B5)
- B7: (Future) Whole-game cross-discipline optimization
- B8: (Future) Automated iteration loop

Key design: each discipline runner and each mutation test gets its own
git worktree on a dedicated branch, enabling parallel execution and
safe config modification. Results are committed to branches for audit.

Also adds vision/roadmap improvement suggestions file.

Co-authored-by: GitHub Copilot <copilot@github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Skip alternate_span in formatted buffer when animations are disabled (#234)

The snake animation (`python` command easter egg) was applying
`set_alternative_span` to formatted buffer parts even when
`--disable-animations` was set, bypassing the intent of that flag.

## Change

- Added `!self.settings.disable_animations` guard to the snake animation
condition in `create_content`, so the `else` branch clears any
alternative span instead:

```rust
// Before
if self.mode.is_running() && part.normal_span().content.starts_with("python") {

// After
if self.mode.is_running() && !self.settings.disable_animations && part.normal_span().content.starts_with("python") {
```

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT TIPS -->
---

- `targets` reduced to a single value: `demos`
- Removed the three per-target `*.output=...` lines from `set:`

commit.message:
Improve subscriber timeout error: use SubscriptionTimeoutException instead of AbruptTerminationException (#2645) (#2727)

When subscriber-timeout fires on a FanoutProcessor with CancelTermination mode,
the actor now calls fail() with a SubscriptionTimeoutException instead of directly
calling context.stop(self). This provides a clear error message ('Subscription
timeout expired, no subscriber attached') rather than a generic
'Processor actor terminated abruptly' from AbruptTerminationException.

A warning log is also emitted when the timeout fires.

References: https://github.com/apache/pekko/issues/2645

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix Java 25 stream test timeout failures (#2573) (#2728)

Motivation: JDK 25 ForkJoinPool scheduling changes cause nightly CI
stream tests to fail with timeouts — ~100+ failures per night.

Modifications:
- Increase timefactor from 2 to 3 for JDK 25+ in nightly CI workflow
- Add 30s PatienceConfig to FlowFlatMapConcatParallelismSpec (100K elements)
- Add 30s PatienceConfig to HubSpec (20K element long-stream tests)
- Increase MapAsyncPartitionedSpec patience from 5s to 15s
- Increase AggregateWithBoundarySpec Await timeouts from 10s to 30s

Result: Timing-sensitive stream tests have sufficient timeout headroom
on JDK 25, reducing false failures in nightly CI.

References: https://github.com/apache/pekko/issues/2573

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Save git commit hash in model metadata (#176)

Adds the current git commit hash to the model save data so we can
trace which version of the code a model was trained on. The hash is
captured at save time and restored on load, and is included in
load_metadata() results.

commit.message:
fix: remove stale ports from persistent tunnel on port change

When PORT env var changes, old ports remained registered on the
persistent tunnel. devtunnel host serves all registered ports, so
the old port URL returned 502 (nothing listening).

Now ensurePersistentTunnel() lists existing ports and removes any
that don't match the current port before hosting.

- Add listPorts() to parse devtunnel port list output
- Call removePort() for stale ports in ensurePersistentTunnel()
- Add 4 new tests for stale port cleanup and listPorts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add 17 focused win/loss encounter tests (9 win + 8 loss):
- Combat, Mining, Herbalism, Woodcutting, Fishing: win + loss

Each test verifies encounter outcome and ability to pick next encounter.

commit.message:
fix: filter ghost sessions without events.jsonl

Copilot CLI creates workspace.yaml on session start but only writes
events.jsonl after the user sends a message. Sessions where the user
exits immediately leave orphaned workspace.yaml files (52 out of 197).

Filter these ghost sessions by requiring events.jsonl to exist,
matching the behavior of copilot --resume picker.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: scouting mutation generates 3 mutated encounter variations (#41)

After completing an encounter, the scouting phase now generates 3 mutated
variations of the just-completed encounter as additional choices alongside
the normal encounter deck refill. Each variation applies a random difficulty
delta ([-0.15, +0.30]) to encounter parameters and mutates ~20% of enemy
deck cards while preserving total card count.

Key changes:
- New scouting.rs module with mutation logic for all 8 disciplines
- GameState captures last_encounter_kind in all discipline finish functions
- EncounterApplyScouting handler generates mutations + normal deck refill
- EncounterPickEncounter handler cleans up un-selected mutation cards
- 3 mutation operations: ScaleValues, RedistributeCopies, SwapTier
- Combat merges 3 decks for 20% selection; herbalism uses characteristic
  mutations; fishing also scales FishCard.value
- Updated tutorial, hints, and designer docs with scouting mutation info
- 3 new integration tests (136 total, 82.39% coverage)

Co-authored-by: GitHub Copilot <copilot@github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: my_little_cardgame
repository.description: Everything is a deck!

commit.message:
Milestone reuse discipline effects (#40)

* BREAKING: Milestones reuse discipline card effects with best values

Milestone encounters no longer define their own separate card effects.
Instead, they look up the discipline's existing EnemyCardEffects and use
roll_best_concrete_effect (max values) to create the most powerful
version of each effect.

Key changes:
- Add tier: u32 field to LibraryCard for tier-aware effect lookups
- Add roll_best_concrete_effect() that always picks max values
- Add Library methods: card_effects_for_discipline_and_tier,
  enemy_effects_for_discipline_and_tier, add_card_with_tier
- Refactor all 5 register_*_milestone functions to derive from
  the discipline's base encounter definition instead of hardcoding
- Combat milestones classify enemy effects into deck categories
- Gathering milestones (mining/herbalism/woodcutting/fishing) extract
  the base encounter structure and rebuild with best-rolled effects
- generate_milestone_reward_effects now also scales EnemyCardEffects
  and tags new effects with the next tier number

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add vision/roadmap suggestions for milestone effect reuse

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Preserve milestone deck composition on tier escalation

When a player beats a milestone and the next tier is generated, the new
milestone now mirrors the defeated milestone's deck structure instead of
rebuilding from scratch or from the base encounter.

Key changes:
- Add Library::replace_card() for in-place card replacement at same ID
- Generalize tier_effect_mapping() to accept from_tier/to_tier
- All 5 register_*_milestone functions accept optional previous def
- Combat milestone preserves per-deck card types and hand counts
- Non-combat milestones derive from defeated milestone (not base encounter)
- handle_milestone_finish extracts defeated inner encounter before replace
- Use scale_factor() consistently for tier scaling ratios
- Add 2 integration tests: deck composition + card ID preservation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: GitHub Copilot <copilot@github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
BREAKING: Add interference deck to research encounters (#39)

Add enemy interference deck to research encounters following the
established enemy deck pattern (OreCard, FishCard, etc.). The deck
has 5 cards in hand and auto-plays one random card per round after
the player's probe, adding difficulty to experiments.

Interference card effects:
- BlockBestMatch (2x): nullifies the player's best-scoring card
- SwapHiddenSlots (1x): permanently swaps two hidden slot symbols
- ReduceYield (1x): subtracts 20-80 from round yield
- ShuffleHiddenSlots (1x): randomly permutes all hidden slot positions

Implementation details:
- New ResearchInterferenceKind enum and InterferenceCard struct
- New CardEffectKind::ResearchInterference variant
- JSON-externalized definitions in configurations/research/cards.json
- Config types (ResearchDefConfig, InterferenceDeckEntryConfig) and
  build_research_def() loader following mining's pattern
- Borrow-checker-safe staged approach in apply_research_interference()
- 2 new integration tests (133 total, 81.72% coverage)
- Updated hints.rs and designer.rs documentation

Co-authored-by: GitHub Copilot <copilot@github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: security hardening — command injection, XSS, upload validation

Command injection (#2 Critical):
- Replace execSync + string interpolation with execFileSync + array args
- Remove shell: true from all devtunnel command executions
- Add DEVTUNNEL_ID validation (alphanumeric + hyphens only)
- Add validateTunnelId() with tests for shell metacharacter rejection

XSS (#3 High):
- Escape preview URLs with AppUtils.escapeHtml() before innerHTML insertion
- Fix in both dashboard.js and app.js

Upload restriction (#6 Medium):
- Restrict file uploads to image extensions only (.png, .jpg, .jpeg, .gif, .webp)
- Return 400 error for disallowed file types

commit.message:
feat: consolidate tunnels, remove anonymous access, change default port to 4117

Security: Remove --allow-anonymous from tunnel creation. Dev Tunnels
account authentication is now required for all access.

Tunnel consolidation: Preview ports now use the main tunnel instead
of spawning separate devtunnel processes. Ports are dynamically
added/removed via devtunnel port create/delete.

Port change: Default server port changed from 3000 to 4117 to avoid
conflicts with common dev tools (React, Express, etc.).

- tunnel.js: Remove --allow-anonymous, add getTunnelId/addPort/removePort
- preview-manager.js: Replace spawn-based approach with port management
- index.js: Change default PORT to 4117
- Update all tests and documentation

* Add navigation test harness

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Implement OCCT solid navigation mapping

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix SurfaceNormal location mismatch and CreatePolyhedron deflection scaling (#6)

* Initial plan

* fix: correct SurfaceNormal location mixing and use relative meshing deflection

- SurfaceNormal: obtain surface + location via BRep_Tool::Surface(face, loc),
  transform query point into surface local frame before GeomAPI_ProjectPointOnSurf
  so the (u,v) parameters match the BRepAdaptor_Surface domain used by
  TryGetOutwardNormal; delegate final normal computation to that helper.
  Remove the now-unused kMinNormalMagnitudeSquared constant.

- CreatePolyhedron: replace hard-coded kLinearDeflection = 0.1 with
  kRelativeDeflection = 0.01 and isRelative=true so mesh density scales
  with each face's bounding-box size rather than being a fixed world length.

Co-authored-by: wdconinc <4656391+wdconinc@users.noreply.github.com>

- 13 unit tests for classification, parsing, and CPU calculation

- SplashView: reduce 'scanning' text delay from 5s to 1.5s
- MusicLibraryService: add comment explaining CAF format support
- AppViewModel: extract splash duration as named constant splashDuration
- NowPlayingView: implement lyrics display when available (showLyrics state used)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add complete native macOS SwiftUI iPod Classic emulator (ClassipodSwift)

Implements a full-featured native macOS SwiftUI application that replicates the Flutter-based iPod Classic emulator with identical visuals and functionality.

## Structure
- ClassipodSwift/ - Standalone Xcode project (no Flutter deps)

## Key Features
- iPod Classic device frame with exact dimensions (443×728) and 12 color options
- Authentic click wheel with circular drag gesture (clockwise/CCW scroll), center select, and 4 cardinal buttons (MENU/prev/play-pause/next)
- Full navigation stack (18 screen types) driven by click wheel
- Music library scanner via AVFoundation (MP3/M4A/AAC/FLAC/WAV/AIFF)
- AVPlayer-based audio player with shuffle, repeat, seek, and queue
- Now Playing screen with artwork, progress bar, and transport controls
- Cover Flow view with 3D-tilted album art
- Settings with live device color switching, theme, and music folder picker
- Battery indicator via IOKit, status bar with playback state
- Security-scoped bookmarks for sandbox-compliant music folder access
- Window with hidden title bar, aspect-ratio lock, hover-reveal traffic lights

## Files Created (28 Swift files + project)
- App/ClassipodSwiftApp.swift, AppDelegate.swift
- Models/Song, AlbumModel, PlaylistModel, AppSettings
- Services/MusicLibraryService, AudioPlayerService, SettingsService
- ViewModels/AppViewModel
- Views/ContentView, SplashView, DeviceFrameView, ClickWheelView,
  ScreenView, MenuListView, StatusBarView, BatteryIndicatorView,
  NowPlayingView, SettingsView, CoverFlowView, SearchView,
  AboutView, DeviceColorSelectionView, ViewExtensions
- Resources/Assets.xcassets, Info.plist
- ClassipodSwift.entitlements
- ClassipodSwift.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
